narrative: Fire ecology work this cycle spans intraspecific trait variation driving serotiny in Yunnan pine and evidence that prescribed burning leaves pine processionary moth resistance largely unchanged, while patch-burn grazing reshaped grassland invertebrate communities through habitat heterogeneity. Climate-linked studies showed grassland fine-root traits buffering repeated drought via species turnover rather than trait plasticity, seasonal timing determining how warming reshapes mast-driven food webs, and 35 years of data revealing drought-intensified competition between livestock and wild herbivores. Movement ecology advanced with an intuitive utilization-distribution method built on step-selection analysis and a fully three-dimensional extension of step-selection functions for animals that swim or fly. Conservation highlights include a three-decade IUCN Red List trajectory for sharks, rays and chimaeras and a rare five-year evaluation of structured expert elicitation within the NSW Saving our Species program. Methodological progress included deep-learning approaches for transferring joint species-distribution models and a new R package inferring forest structure from remote sensing, while biogeography and evolution contributions examined climatic niche shifts in invasive plants and what climate adaptation practice can learn from evolutionary theory.

articles.title: Pinus yunnanensis var. pygmaea individuals are more ready to embrace wildfire in more fire‐prone areas
